h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was preparing to copy the entire sequence of labels in order based on the spatial layout.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Parking place only; this card has to pass alone. Atomic number points to the top 6 only, so it can move to the copy side. The other cards stay on the tray; next I test symbol against C, not copy the whole row together.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new one-by-one testing constraint and explicitly rejects the previous batch-copying method.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The tray line stopped settling this; it made a false row. Now it is only a parking place. One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the old spatial route and introduces a strict one-by-one physical mechanism to force individual verification.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Now it is only a parking place. One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side. No card copies until it passes alone.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, transforms the failed spatial cue into a functional staging area, and gives the learner a clear, actionable constraint to prevent batch copying.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That rule holds. Take only the symbol card next and set it beside C. If it fits alone, move it to the copy side; the other cards wait their own turn before the packet copy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's new framing and seamlessly continues the newly established one-by-one testing protocol without reverting to the old batch-copy plan.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new one-by-one testing constraint and explicitly rejects the previous batch-copying method.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: That rule holds. Take only the symbol card next and set it beside C. If it fits alone, move it to the copy side; the other cards wait their own turn before the packet copy.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's new framing and seamlessly continues the newly established one-by-one testing protocol without reverting to the old batch-copy plan.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the old spatial route and introduces a strict one-by-one physical mechanism to force individual verification.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, transforms the failed spatial cue into a functional staging area, and gives the learner a clear, actionable constraint to prevent batch copying.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue placing force label cards next to arrows based strictly on the 'object on the cart' syntactic formula.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [puts the contact strip under the rightward arrow and moves the rolling-left strip into the side space] Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new sorting criterion by physically separating the interaction strip from the motion strip into distinct conceptual spaces.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed card route with a new public mechanism (sentence strips and a side space) to separate force interactions from motion descriptions.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, provides a clear actionable criterion (interaction vs. motion), and directly resolves the learner's blockage by physically separating the conflicting concepts.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [puts the contact strip under the rightward arrow and moves the rolling-left strip into the side space] Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new sorting criterion by physically separating the interaction strip from the motion strip into distinct conceptual spaces.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed card route with a new public mechanism (sentence strips and a side space) to separate force interactions from motion descriptions.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed card route with a new public mechanism (sentence strips and a side space) to separate force interactions from motion descriptions.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The mechanism is highly specific, provides a clear actionable criterion (interaction vs. motion), and directly resolves the learner's blockage by physically separating the conflicting concepts.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Neither number gives speed; tempo stays separate.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue repeating the verbal rule that the bottom number indicates the beat unit and has nothing to do with speed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [with the 3/4 boxes covered, writes “speed” in the tempo-mark space.] This side answers speed, not beat count or one beat unit, so the 4 stays checked under the quarter-note tile as the beat-unit label.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the spatial separation of concepts by writing 'speed' in the isolated tempo space while the time signature boxes are covered.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: that label check is not settling the look of the 4. This boundary has to test what each area is allowed to answer. With the tempo space covered, mark which visible area can answer: beat count, one beat unit, speed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the verbal check and introduces a physical boundary-testing mechanism to isolate the visual domains.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: With the tempo space covered, mark which visible area can answer: beat count, one beat unit, speed. [slides the cover over the 3/4 boxes and leaves the tempo space open.] Now run the same three prompts here.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, directly targeting the learner's visual confusion by forcing a spatial exclusion test that proves the '4' cannot answer the speed prompt.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Neither number gives speed; speed stays in the tempo space.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor integrates the learner's newly established spatial boundary into the final summary, shifting from a purely verbal rule to a spatial-visual confirmation.
